checks.cmd: python3 -m py_compile scripts/context/phases/impact.py scripts/context/__init__.py scripts/context/storage.py scripts/workflow_memory.py
checks.name: lint

patternCompliance.detail: All new code uses only stdlib imports (collections, dataclasses, argparse, sqlite3)
graph-* commands follow same argparse + dispatch pattern as existing commands
All 3 tasks followed RED-GREEN cycle; 34 new tests (13+9+12)

performanceFindings.detail: BFS impact_analysis uses visited set for cycle detection; SQL queries use indexed columns (file_path, source, target, type)

principleNotes.detail: BFS implementation is ~60 LOC; CLI handlers are thin wrappers delegating to ContextGraph methods
Only files in plan scope were modified; no drive-by refactors
All 3 plan tasks verified with fresh test evidence

evidence: Plan goal: 'Implement impact analysis (BFS blast radius) and expose the context graph via CLI commands' — all 3 tasks delivered
Task 1: impact.py created with ImpactResult + impact_analysis(), wired into ContextGraph.impact() — 13 tests pass
Task 2: get_related_nodes() added, context() implemented with full neighborhood — 9 tests pass
Task 3: 4 CLI subcommands (graph-index/query/impact/context) with --repo flag, bypassing memory DB check — 12 tests pass
Changed files match plan scope exactly (plus test_context_graph.py for stale stub updates)
stageReviews.notes: All 3 tasks complete. Plan scope respected. Only pre-existing warnings from earlier plans.

findings.detail: Accesses storage._conn directly for rel/file counts. Minor — stats could be added to GraphStorage API later.
Accepts returncode 0 or 1 — soft assertion. Acceptable for integration test where node ID format may vary.

evidence: No security findings (no user input beyond CLI args, parameterized SQL throughout)
BFS uses visited set preventing infinite loops
CLI commands properly close() graph in finally blocks
graph commands correctly exempt from memory engine DB check
stageReviews.notes: Clean implementation. Two info-level observations, no blockers.

scoring.notes: Operational readiness at 1/2: CLI works but no --json output flag on graph commands for machine consumption. Non-blocking.
